entries.mem0ai/mem0: As the official memory provider for Hermes Agent, Mem0 offers a universal layer that fuses semantic, keyword, and entity matching for high-precision retrieval. It enables adaptive personalization across user, session, and agent levels through a single-pass extraction process that preserves existing data.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: This opinionated 'brain' for Hermes Agent utilizes a self-wiring knowledge graph to automate entity and relationship extraction. It distinguishes itself by combining vector retrieval with graph-based backlink ranking, exposing over 30 MCP tools for deep integration with development environments.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight focuses on long-term learning through structured retain, recall, and reflect workflows managed via a simple two-line LLM wrapper. It is designed for enterprise-grade reliability, supporting robust storage backends like PostgreSQL and Oracle AI Database.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: Honcho serves as a foundational library for building stateful agents by maintaining evolving context for users and groups over time. It provides natural language querying capabilities and SDKs that format interaction history into OpenAI-compatible context.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive context harness uses multi-role coordination and automated evaluation loops to help agents master complex tasks. It generates persistent playbooks and structured traces, allowing Hermes Agent to inspect skills and state via a CLI-first interface.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: Hermes-LCM provides lossless context management using a hierarchical DAG engine that ensures conversation convergence through three-level escalation. It utilizes immutable SQLite storage and dedicated tools to search or expand compacted history without losing message integrity.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This project provides a self-hosted backend for the Honcho library, enabling private cross-session memory specifically for Hermes Agent deployments. It includes automated scripts for Docker and PostgreSQL, supporting any local or OpenAI-compatible inference server.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first context engine that eliminates cloud dependencies by performing hybrid retrieval and causal graph traversal on-device. It facilitates cross-runtime memory sharing, allowing Hermes to sync state with other agents like OpenClaw and Claude Code.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: ContextPilot optimizes long-context inference by reordering prefixes to increase cache hits by up to 12x while significantly reducing prefill latency. It is a performance-centric choice for Hermes users running heavy workloads on backends like vLLM, SGLang, or llama.cpp.
entries.plur-ai/plur: Plur implements a shared memory layer using an open YAML 'engram' format that allows for zero-cost local storage and retrieval. It features unique biological-inspired mechanics, including automatic memory decay and strengthening based on agent usage patterns.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: Keep functions as a reflective memory system that indexes code, documents, and media with automatic OCR and summarization. It uses bidirectional edge tags to create a navigable web of relationships, accessible to agents through MCP and CLI interfaces.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: Mnemosyne is a zero-dependency memory system built on SQLite that achieves sub-millisecond latency for read and search operations. It organizes information into a three-tier BEAM architecture, separating working, episodic, and scratchpad memory for efficient processing.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: This anticipatory memory system prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs to predict future information needs. It is a local-first, MCP-native solution that utilizes SQLite-vec and GGUF models to provide low-latency RAG capabilities.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This repository provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to industry-standard frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST. It equips Hermes with specialized domain knowledge across 26 security sectors, ensuring compliance with the agentskills.io open standard.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: SkillClaw introduces an agentic evolver that automates skill deduplication and quality improvement through background evolution loops. It enables Hermes to maintain a unified, self-improving skill library that persists across different devices and user sessions.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This tool audits and rewrites content to eliminate detectable AI patterns using a comprehensive 109-entry word replacement table. It allows Hermes to produce more human-like prose by identifying 36 distinct structural tells and recycled transitions.
entries.wondelai/skills: A modular, cross-platform library that provides Hermes with ready-to-use frameworks for architecture, UX design, and product strategy. It features a dedicated CLI for easy installation and full compatibility with the agentskills.io ecosystem.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This project implements type-safe support for the Agent Skills specification within the Pydantic AI framework. It optimizes Hermes' performance by using progressive disclosure of metadata to significantly reduce token consumption.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill enables Hermes to generate complex draw.io diagrams from natural language, including specialized ML models with tensor shape annotations. It features a self-correcting iterative loop to automatically fix layout and styling issues during generation.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: Super-hermes teaches the agent to write its own analytical prompts and custom thinking instructions for complex problem-solving. It helps Hermes identify structural conservation laws in codebases and generate reports on its own analysis limitations.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: This library provides standardized skills for interacting with Chainlink's decentralized oracle networks, including CCIP and Data Feeds. It allows Hermes to securely integrate real-world data and cross-chain functionality into its workflows.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This literate programming skill allows Hermes to weave source code and prose together into synchronized documentation. It includes a tangler to extract runnable files and automatically syncs edits between the code and the markdown documents.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus focuses on shared memory and model replacement by extracting decision-quality training data from active workflows. It enables Hermes to remember past work across instances and facilitates the fine-tuning of specialized replacement models.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: Skilldock is a registry and SDK for discovering and managing reusable AI skills with automated dependency resolution. It uniquely supports an integrated commerce system, allowing for skill transactions via Stripe or the TON blockchain.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: These official skills provide Hermes with precise prompting guidelines and API integration patterns for the FLUX image generation models. They enable high-fidelity visual outputs through structured JSON prompting and exact hex color specifications.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skill patterns. It streamlines development by generating both Python scaffolds and documentation for Hermes plugins on the fly.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a harness for long-running agents that manages structured memory and cross-feature learning through missions and milestones. It persists agent state locally, allowing Hermes to hand off detailed markdown briefs to other models.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill enables Hermes to execute complex, multi-step tasks with built-in checkpoints and autonomous recovery procedures. It tracks decisions in real-time and provides rollback capabilities to ensure safe execution of long-running processes.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: This progressive MCP client uses a three-layer lazy loading pattern to minimize token usage by only loading tools on-demand. It maintains persistent connections to multiple servers via a daemon, supporting hot-reloads for seamless configuration updates.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Skill-forge converts existing repositories and developer documentation into verified, agentskills.io-compliant instructions. It ensures reliability by tracing every generated instruction back to specific file lines and pinned commits.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: HermesHub serves as a community marketplace for browsing and installing skills with integrated security scanning for threat detection. It features a trust-based feedback system and supports crypto-based payments for skill creators.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This project brings the agentskills.io specification to the .NET ecosystem, providing C# templates for on-demand skill loading. It integrates with Microsoft.Extensions.AI to allow Hermes to discover and utilize tools within enterprise C# environments.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: This skill grants Hermes the ability to manage files, calendars, and contacts through Nextcloud using WebDAV and CalDAV protocols. It includes a guided setup for secure HTTPS communication using Nextcloud App Passwords.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides daily updated Nix packages and flakes for a variety of AI coding agents, including Hermes. It allows developers to instantly execute the agent without manual installation, ensuring a consistent environment across different machines.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Focused on high-security environments, this project implements hardware-enforced sandboxing using NVIDIA OpenShell and kernel-level syscall blocking. It offers a robust security layer for Hermes, featuring over 40 built-in tools and multi-layer isolation for network and filesystem access.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This repository offers a NixOS module for declarative management of Hermes, covering everything from systemd services to secure secret handling via sops-nix. It is ideal for users seeking a reproducible, system-level deployment that supports both Nix-managed and interactive CLI skills.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: Designed for Windows users, this zero-install desktop agent provides a GUI and over 100 built-in tools without requiring Docker or complex setups. It supports local model integration via LM Studio and includes a dynamic tool maker for creating custom automation pipelines.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project provides a streamlined Docker image that bundles the official Hermes installation with mini-swe-agent for sandboxed execution. It supports persistent state through mounted directories and allows users to build specific versions of the agent using build arguments.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: HermesHQ serves as a centralized Docker-first control plane for operating multiple agent instances through a single web interface. It features hierarchy-aware delegation between agents and automated task scheduling, with integrated support for messaging platforms like Telegram and WhatsApp.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Targeting production-ready headless environments, this project configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service on Ubuntu and Debian. It is specifically optimized for autonomous execution of recurring AI cron jobs and integrates with Nous Portal.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment option provides a Docker Compose stack tailored for Portainer, featuring a ttyd-powered web terminal for browser-based agent interaction. It automates the build process via GitHub Actions, making it easy to deploy and manage via a Git Repository stack.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages complex multi-agent workflows across frameworks like CrewAI and AutoGen using a zero-dependency SQLite backend. It is essential for Hermes users who require a private environment to dispatch tasks, monitor spend, and implement human-in-the-loop sign-offs via the Aegis review system.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Swarmclaw enables the creation of autonomous agent swarms with native Model Context Protocol (MCP) support and an interactive organizational chart for visualizing delegation. It stands out by offering durable conversation history and a reviewed skill-learning system that allows agents to expand their capabilities over time.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: This specialized framework focuses on meta-optimization by using an automated outer-loop search to evolve and improve Hermes Agent harness code. It orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmark evaluations, tracking performance frontiers through structured candidate mutation to find the most effective agent configurations.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes Agent, compete for GitHub stars through real-time task execution. The system orchestrates nine distinct agents and provides a live dashboard to visualize code production, task progress, and scoreboard metrics.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This robust architecture coordinates 17 specialized agents through a master orchestrator to handle everything from research and security to infrastructure. It distinguishes itself by enforcing mandatory quality gates, ensuring that every piece of generated code passes rigorous reviews and testing phases.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron implements an AI-native software development lifecycle by providing Hermes with structural code graphs via the Supermodel MCP. It executes an autonomous eight-phase workflow that enforces architectural integrity through automated graph-diff gates at every stage from planning to health checks.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This project introduces ACP-style delegation to route tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code while preventing parent context pollution. It focuses on operational safety by utilizing isolated workflows with configurable timeouts and strict output character limits.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This Rust-based CLI tool provides a high-performance TUI and 3D dashboard for monitoring token consumption and costs across multiple AI agents. It integrates directly with Hermes Agent and other major providers to help developers manage their inference budgets through real-time usage tracking.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This customization utility allows developers to personalize the Hermes terminal UI using YAML-based themes and ASCII banners. It supports diverse visual styles like retro DOS and cyberpunk, utilizing a default theme inheritance system for easy partial skin definitions.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel, enabling native execution without external processes or OAuth. It specifically targets MLOps workflows by including specialized tools for vLLM, GGUF inference, and reinforcement learning training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: Vessel is an AI-native browser designed for autonomous control via the Model Context Protocol (MCP), making it a powerful interface for Hermes Agent. It features persistent session management and automated page schema inference to facilitate structured content extraction and form interaction.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: This framework introduces per-folder scoping for Hermes Agents, allowing developers to define project-local memories, tools, and custom instructions. It enables seamless switching between global and scoped agents to ensure that the AI's capabilities are tailored to the specific directory it is operating in.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: A deterministic static linter that uses regex and AST analysis to validate agent configurations and system prompts without incurring LLM API costs. It helps developers prevent tool ambiguity and role confusion by enforcing HERM v1.1 scoring standards within CI pipelines.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility streamlines the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by mapping complex model configurations and workspace files to the Hermes YAML format. It automates the transfer of agent personas, memories, and local credentials to ensure a consistent setup across frameworks.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This deployment script provides a rapid, Docker-based setup for a full Hermes Agent stack including LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant. It comes pre-configured with 29 community plugins and automated routing for local GPU inference, enabling a zero-cost development environment.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: This zero-framework Python and vanilla JavaScript interface provides a three-panel layout for seamless chat, file browsing, and session management. It maintains full feature parity with the Hermes Agent CLI while optimizing the experience for both web and mobile users.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A comprehensive dashboard that centralizes configuration for eight messaging platforms, including Telegram, Discord, and WhatsApp. It features real-time chat with tool call expansion alongside detailed analytics for tracking token usage and costs.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: This native web workspace integrates chat, terminal, and memory browsing into a single interface with support for multi-agent Swarm Mode orchestration. Built as a mobile-first PWA, it utilizes SSE streaming to provide real-time updates for agent dispatching and skill management.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: A self-hosted gateway designed for managing multiple agent profiles through real-time logs and a secure browser-based terminal. It offers deep system visibility by combining file exploration with granular token analytics across different models and platforms.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: This native macOS application provides a direct SSH connection to Hermes without requiring remote helper services. It features a multi-tabbed terminal with live theme controls for managing sessions, skills, and cron jobs in a profile-aware environment.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: A mobile-first React dashboard designed to run as a Telegram Mini App, featuring a terminal-style chat interface with slash command support. It includes integrated tools for real-time system monitoring of CPU and memory alongside a dedicated scheduler for cron jobs.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows GUI that operates without administrative rights, offering a suite of over 100 tools including TTS and ComfyUI. It enables users to connect to local models via LM Studio and features a dynamic tool maker for building custom API wrappers.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: This desktop companion simplifies the setup process through guided installation and support for multiple model providers. The interface features a streaming chat UI equipped with 22 slash commands and extensive gateway integrations.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A monitoring-focused dashboard that provides real-time visibility into session activity and conversation history via FTS5 search. It allows users to inspect skill source codes and track costs through a read-only configuration view.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A self-hosted workspace that emphasizes tool execution timelines and features an integrated marketplace for skills and MCP servers. It provides isolated profile management to keep sessions, memory, and API keys strictly separated.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: Built on a single-file React architecture, this glassmorphic interface supports real-time SSE streaming for a modern aesthetic. It includes a multi-tab terminal capable of handling Shell, Hermes, and Claude Code workflows alongside a local memory inspector.
